title: Methods should not have too many overloads
messageFormat: Method '{0}' has {1} overloads, above the limit of {2}.
summary: A large family of overloads for one method name pushes the burden of choosing the right entry point onto every caller and tends to hide subtle behavioural differences between near-identical signatures. Counting the overloads of each name surfaces APIs that might be better served by optional parameters, a builder, or a single options object.
